Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: this is a detailed DECLARE model analysis request The user wants a practical interpretation of each activity and constraint plus how they interconnect to ensure compliance and process integrity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to map each DECLARE constraint type to real world logic Existence absence constraints are straightforward mandatory vs prohibited steps Exactly one ensures no redundant checks The response succession chains define the core workflow sequence The alternative and chain variants handle parallel or conditional paths Non constraints prevent invalid combinations

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user also wants business regulatory context I should highlight how each constraint maps to common loan industry requirements anti fraud no disbursement before application consumer protection quality reviews and audit trails exactly one checks The Proceed Without Compliance absence constraint is particularly interesting it is a hard stop for regulatory violations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The business goals likely revolve around risk mitigation and regulatory adherence Truth in Lending Act Basel norms

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the interplay analysis I will emphasize how constraints create fail safes eg noncoexistence between Transfer Funds and Receive Application prevents premature disbursement while chainprecedence ensures credit checks validate before contract authorization

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will structure the response by constraint category then synthesize into process integrity and business rationale The tone should be analytical but accessible avoiding academic jargon since the user seems familiar with DECLARE but wants practical insights
